concerns.text: FR-008 anchors to 'See Assumptions' rather than a specific User Story. While the Assumptions section mentions compute constraints, the requirement describes a system behavior (enforcing caps, subsampling) that should be explicitly anchored to a User Story (likely US-1 or US-3) to ensure traceability. The current anchor is indirect.

SC-004 anchors to 'See Assumptions' rather than a specific User Story. The success criterion (memory footprint) is a constraint on the system's execution, which should be explicitly linked to the User Story that defines the processing capability (US-1) to avoid orphaned success criteria.

SC-005 anchors to 'See Assumptions' rather than a specific User Story. Similar to SC-004, the runtime limit is a constraint on the pipeline execution. It should be explicitly anchored to the relevant User Story (US-1 or US-3) to maintain strict story-to-criterion traceability.
